title: HMPS 2026
content:
BPD Healthcare will be at HMPS this year—find us at Booth #32. Our EVP of Strategy & Innovation will join a panel, and we’re hosting a happy hour to continue the AI conversations sparked at this year’s Joe Public Retreat. Let’s connect and shape the future of healthcare together.

May 5, 2026, 10:15 AM - 11:15 AM 

The AI Dream: Building the Future of Healthcare Marketing and Experience

AI is an enterprise-wide shift that is redefining how healthcare organizations work, decide, and communicate. Join marketing, digital, and communications leaders as they share how they are adopting AI, establishing governance, and managing change as they move from experimentation to meaningful impact through cross-functional collaboration.

Featured Speakers

  • Stephanie Wierwille, BPD Healthcare, EVP, Strategy & Innovation
  • Jeremy Rogers, Indiana University Health, VP, Digital Marketing & Experience

The AI Dream: Joe Public Happy Hour

May 4, 2026 | 6-8 PM

You asked—we’re making it happen.  Join us at HMPS in Salt Lake City this May as we bring the group back together. We’re hosting a JPR 2026 happy hour so attendees can reconnect, swap updates, and share how things are taking shape. Can’t wait to see everyone in one room again.

content:
April 30, 2026, 11:00 AM

The Marketing Funnel vs. Real Life

Relevance isn't won in a single moment; it's built over time, with messages and solutions that are delivered at the speed of life. Join Mindy Adams, Chief Creative Officer at BPD Healthcare, as we talk through the consumer's heart space and mindset: meeting people where they are, listening to what's on their mind and understanding when they're ready to take action.

Let's Talk

The headlines around AI primarily focus on saving time and costs. And in the rush to automate, we’ve noticed the conversation is focused almost exclusively on efficiency and effectiveness.
This paper aims to inspire marketers to dream bigger. It’s an invitation to ask, “What can we do that’s never been done before?” To answer that question, it helps to first imagine what that future could look like.

title: AI in Healthcare Marketing Week Sponsored by BPD
content:
Inside the Real-World AI Playbooks of Leading Health Systems

CONTACT US

Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Strategy & Innovation, BPD Amanda Todorovich Enterprise Executive Director, Digital Marketing, Cleveland Clinic Val Lopez AVP, Marketing Operations, Baptist Health South Florida Lisa D. Mason Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights, Providence
Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Strategy & Innovation, BPD LinkedIn
Email: swierwille@bpdhealthcare.com Stephanie Wierwille is the Executive Vice President of Engagement at BPD, where she helps healthcare brands craft and deliver compelling stories across digital, traditional, and emerging channels. With deep expertise in social-first, full-funnel marketing, she guides organizations toward marketing maturity and consumer-first engagement strategies. With a decade of experience spanning healthcare and consumer brands, Stephanie has led digital and content strategy for organizations like MD Anderson Cancer Center, Cincinnati Children’s, Johns Hopkins Medicine, Delta Air Lines, Amazon, and Walmart. From launching TGI Fridays’ first social marketing initiatives to helping Delta reshape its approach to social media across operations, marketing, and corporate communications, she has been at the forefront of digital transformation. Stephanie earned her MBA from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and is an adjunct professor at The University of Oklahoma. Passionate about innovation, she continues to push the boundaries of healthcare marketing, ensuring brands connect with audiences in meaningful and measurable ways.  
Amanda Todorovich Enterprise Executive Director, Digital Marketing, Cleveland Clinic LinkedIn
Amanda Todorovich leads digital marketing for Cleveland Clinic, one of the most trusted healthcare brands in the world. As Enterprise Executive Director, she oversees content strategy, digital experience, SEO, multimedia production, social media, and platform operations. Under her leadership, ClevelandClinic.org has grown to over 1.2 billion annual sessions—driving significant brand awareness, patient acquisition, and more than $15 million in annual advertising revenue. Amanda built and scaled a healthcare publishing model inside a health system—transforming Cleveland Clinic’s digital presence into an industry-defining engine for engagement, revenue, and trust. Her team manages thousands of content assets through an in-house editorial staff and a robust production infrastructure. Their work is not only clinically accurate and HIPAA-compliant, but also search-dominant and brand-aligned. Recognized as one of the most influential voices in healthcare digital marketing, Amanda is a respected thought leader, innovation driver, and key architect of Cleveland Clinic’s global digital growth. She holds a master’s degree in communications from The University of Akron, a bachelor’s degree in public relations from Ohio Northern University, and is a Fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives.  
Val Lopez AVP, Marketing Operations, Baptist Health South Florida LinkedIn
 Email: ValLo@baptisthealth.net Val Lopez serves as the Assistant Vice President of Marketing Operations at Baptist Health South Florida, the region’s largest not-for-profit healthcare system. In this role, he oversees web experience, digital marketing strategy, marketing technology, paid media, marketing automation, personalization, and CRM initiatives that drive patient engagement and organizational growth. With more than 20+ years of experience leading digital marketing teams, Val has played a key role in helping healthcare organizations embrace new technologies and data-driven strategies. Prior to joining Baptist Health, he founded Martek Health, a healthcare digital marketing consultancy focused on helping health systems modernize their marketing operations. He also served as AVP of Digital Marketing at the University of Miami Health System, where he advanced digital strategies to improve patient access and brand visibility. Earlier in his career, Val held leadership roles in the travel and quick service restaurant industries with National Car Rental, Alamo Rent A Car, and Burger King Corporation, experiences that sharpened his expertise in customer experience, digital operations, and marketing innovation across large-scale organizations. Val earned his bachelor’s degree from Florida International University and his MBA from the University of Miami. He is passionate about how AI can help marketing functions reshape the consumer and patient experience in healthcare, ensuring that technology not only advances efficiency but also delivers more personalized and meaningful interactions.  
Lisa D. Mason Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights, Providence LinkedIn
 Email: Lisa.Mason@providence.org Lisa D. Mason is the Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights at Providence working on developing transformative platform technologies for healthcare.  Her organization has launched several health technology companies and designed the Providence Digital Health Platform which serves as the digital front door for patients. Lisa designed and launched one of the 1st LLM-based healthcare messaging chatbot assistants for enhancing care access and reducing provider burden. She has done comprehensive research on how to gain patient trust through chatbot personality & avatar design—all while ensuring patient safety. Lisa is actively working on AI-based workflow automation solutions to transform the efficiency of care delivery by leveraging AI and digital technologies to create seamless, patient-centered, and personalized healthcare experiences. Lisa has more than 25 years of experience in consumer research and product design, leading teams and companies through the complexities of understanding customers and driving product direction and vision. She started her career at Microsoft, covering emerging technologies in travel, video games, consumer and enterprise software, and mobile and consumer hardware. Lisa holds an M.S. in Physics from the University of Minnesota. Download presentation

AI and Content: Turning Generative Tools into a Scalable Strategy

CONTACT US

Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy, BPD Carolina Anthony Digital Marketing Strategist & Community Advocate, AdventHealth Matt Cyr Founder & President, Loop AI Agency Allie Karas Senior Vice President of Content, MJH Life Sciences
Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy, BPD
Email: ebaker@bpdhealthcare.com Emily Baker is a digital marketing leader with more than a decade of experience shaping content and social strategies that drive connection and engagement. A former healthcare business reporter, she brings an investigative approach to storytelling that delivers measurable impact for healthcare organizations, and is advancing initiatives that harness AI to unlock new engagement capabilities and elevate the impact of marketing teams.  
Carolina Anthony Digital Marketing Strategist & Community Advocate, AdventHealth LinkedIn
Email: Carolina Anthony is a passionate digital marketing strategist and community advocate leading omnichannel growth for AdventHealth. With over 18 years of experience, she cultivates digital spaces that amplify messages, build trust, and transform how communities connect with health and wellness. At AdventHealth, Carolina leads content strategy, social media, reputation management, web marketing and digital project management. She’s passionate about elevating patient experience and measurable outcomes across the digital continuum. Prior to this role, she brought her strategic vision to organizations including Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts, Hilton Grand Vacations and Golf Channel/NBC Sports, always leading with an emphasis on storytelling and brand impact. What truly distinguishes Carolina is her commitment to mentorship and inclusion: she builds cross‑functional, diverse teams and champions professional growth so women can lead with confidence and creativity. A certified yoga instructor and lifelong learner, she holds a BA in Communications & Business from Florida State University and an MA from Rollins College, and she weaves these strengths into empowering others. Carolina lives in Orlando with her husband and Mini Schnauzer, Rex. When she’s not transforming digital ecosystems, she’s traveling, exploring wellness, or getting lost in a great book.

He began his career leading digital marketing at Boston Children’s Hospital and Dana-Farber, then guided healthcare clients at Primacy. Through Loop, Matt helps organizations adopt AI thoughtfully — enhancing creativity, collaboration, and trust between humans and technology.  
Allie Karas Senior Vice President of Content, MJH Life Sciences LinkedIn

She leads a 150-person team spanning more than 50 healthcare brands across oncology, specialty care, managed care, and industry sciences. A strategic architect of MJH’s content transformation, Allie is spearheading a movement to pivot away from traditional editorial to a high-impact, multimedia-first, AI-powered content engine model that is redefining how healthcare audiences learn, engage, and stay ahead of the science. Allie is passionate about the intersection of technology, storytelling, and clinical impact, and brings a fresh perspective on how AI can elevate content quality, speed, creativity, and influence across modern healthcare media. Personally, Allie is highly invested in leadership development and coaching the next generation of women leaders. She lives in Pennsylvania with her husband and four children. Download presentation

Overseeing Ochsner’s marketing strategy and technology, Amber manages the organization’s web presence, digital marketing, social media programs, online reputation management, consumer insights and analytics and client relationship management. Her strategic focus is on increasing brand awareness, affinity, and patient acquisition while fostering loyalty through high-value content and seamless digital experiences. With over 15 years of expertise in digital content, healthcare marketing, and technology, Amber thrives on leveraging data, market research, and cutting-edge tools to empower online health seekers to manage their care. She’s embraced AI for marketing, ensuring a consistent brand voice across all channels while enhancing program efficiencies and output across the department. From optimizing user experiences across Ochsner’s websites to executing digital campaigns that integrate with the EMR, her mission is to connect people to Ochsner while providing seamless access to healthcare for the Gulf South’s communities. Prior to joining Ochsner in 2011, Amber worked in journalism and publications with a focus on online content and websites. She received her Master of Science in Journalism from Columbia University and a Bachelor of Arts degree in both English and Spanish from the University of California at Santa Barbara. At UCI Health, Nooteboom oversees the digital consumer journey and experience roadmap. She and her team are responsible for driving access to care, transforming digital experiences, and orchestrating brand experience. Since joining UCI Health in 2022, Nooteboom has been an instrumental player in the organization’s growth and digital transformation. Her leadership enabled the launch of the UCI Health digital front door in February 2023, followed by same-day care digital service navigation and new patient digital appointment access. These included launching the first-ever consumer website with scheduling options for the organization, since expanding to include several specialties and services and over 2000 appointments, as well as expansion of authenticated scheduling options in MyChart. Prior to joining UCI Health, Nooteboom led digital patient engagement at Rush University System for Health in Chicago. She lives in Los Angeles with her two cats, Ingrid Bergman and Humphrey Bogart.  
Josefer Montes Executive Director, Marketing & Branding, AdventHealth LinkedIn

He blends sales expertise, brand strategy, and data-driven storytelling to build trust, support clinicians, and expand access to care. A former professor and academic dean, he now focuses on practice—leading integrated campaigns that pair clinical accuracy with clear, human language. His work spans brand development, demand generation, stakeholder communications and more. Guided by a consumer-first mindset and AdventHealth’s promise to help people feel whole—body, mind, and spirit—he helps deliver measurable, mission-aligned results.  
Micha Siegel Director, Product Innovation and Strategy, BPD LinkedIn

For the past three years, Micha has focused on 1:1 marketing, using data, analytics, and claims insights to create personalized, HIPAA-compliant campaigns. His passion lies in connecting data with human insights to deliver tailored messaging that drives patient engagement and measurable results. Originally from Chicago, and a self-professed lover of deep-dish pizza, he would rather be working out than sitting on his couch and has put this energy into becoming an avid triathlete. As such, he enjoys, in equal measure, running, biking and swimming, and he loves having his daughters join him in these activities. Download presentation

Victor Reiss Chief Consumer Officer, UNC Health LinkedIn
 Email: Victor.Reiss@unchealth.unc.edu As Chief Consumer Officer at UNC Health, Victor Reiss leads the system strategy for consumer insights, growth marketing, brand, community engagement, and communications. His work is guided by a core belief: the future of healthcare will be defined by how we honor people’s time. Every moment returned to patients, families, and communities is an opportunity for healing, connection, and living more fully. Victor brings a thought-leader’s lens to the evolving role of consumers in healthcare—asking not only how people access care, but why their experiences succeed or fall short. He envisions a health system where friction is designed out, where consumer insights guide every decision, and where trust is earned through seamless, intuitive interactions. Prior to this role, Victor served as System Vice President of Growth Marketing and Consumer Insights at UNC Health. title: Becker's CEO & CFO Roundtable 2025
content:
Becker’s Healthcare CEO + CFO Roundtable is where healthcare’s top leaders come together to chart the path forward for our industry. This exclusive event convenes C-level executives for dynamic conversations on the most pressing challenges and opportunities facing health systems today — from rural healthcare and digital transformation to advancing health equity, building C-suite diversity, and more.

BPD is proud to be in attendance, and we can’t wait to meet you there.

November 3, 2025, 1:10 PM - 1:50 PM CT

Don’t Punt on Brand: Why Decisive Brand Strategy in M&A Creates Financial Value and Market Clarity

BPD and BrandActive have worked side by side for years, helping health systems navigate the intersection of brand, value, and transformation. Together, we believe health systems have a powerful, often overlooked opportunity: to uncover strategic, financial, and brand value by integrating brand decisions into M&A exploration and negotiation. When those decisions are made as part of the deal, not after it, health systems can capitalize costs, improve EBITDA, and accelerate brand value.

Learning Objectives

1

Learn why most organizations miss huge opportunities by not making a brand decision during an M/A deal

2

Understand that beyond diluting brand value and cultural cohesion by delaying brand decisions, organizations also miss an opportunity to improve their EBITDA by millions

3

Learn the value of developing a brand strategy as part of your M/A strategy, and how to leverage that brand strategy as part of your M/A deals

Featured Speakers

Nicole James Senior Vice President, Communications Philip Guiliano Founding Partner, BrandActive
Nicole James Senior Vice President, Communications

How marketing a brand identity decision during an M/A deal can save millions in EBITDA.

Health system leaders are always on the lookout for ideas and strategies from outside the industry to advance their organization. Here’s one – a strategy that builds a stronger culture, improves reputation and market share, and could add millions in EBIDTA. And all it takes is making one key decision at the right time. title: HCIC 2025
content:
HCIC is the premier conference for healthcare marketers and digital leaders. The strategic roadmap where marketing, digital innovation, and consumer experience intersect.

We’ll be there—let’s connect to collaborate and shape the future of healthcare together.

The Great Social Media Shake-Up: What’s Next for Healthcare Marketing?

Social media is in a state of constant disruption—and healthcare brands are feeling the impact. Organic reach is declining, platforms are shifting, and consumer behaviors are evolving faster than ever. With major algorithm changes, regulatory scrutiny, and new AI-driven content strategies, what worked last year may already be obsolete. So where do health systems go from here? In this session, we’ll unpack the biggest social media shifts happening right now and what they mean for healthcare marketers. From the rise of private communities and social search to AI-powered content and the death of ‘business as usual’ on major platforms, we’ll explore where to focus your time, budget, and energy in 2025. Hospital Reputation Attacks – What’s Trending and How do We Combat it?

Hospitals and health systems have long been targeted by negative narratives that portray them as profit-driven entities prioritizing revenue over patient care. As insurance premiums rise and administrative burdens increase, blame for healthcare costs has shifted to hospitals. This narrative, fueled by media campaigns, academic research, and external pressures, has eroded consumer trust, with many believing hospitals care more about profits than patients. With mounting political and reputational risks, it's time for hospitals to unite and defend their commitment to quality patient care.

Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer, BPD Victor Reiss Chief Consumer Officer, UNC Health Matt Gove Chief Growth Officer, QuickMD
Chris Bevolo Chief Transformation Officer, BPD

Chris Bevolo is the Chief Transformation Officer at BPD, where he helps healthcare’s leading brands reimagine their roles, reinvent their businesses, and transform their impact on the industry. Prior to joining BPD, he served as CEO of Revive, a healthcare agency acquired by BPD in 2023.  

For more than 25 years, Chris has advised hospitals, health systems, and healthcare organizations on strategy, branding, marketing, digital innovation, and change management. An award-winning author of seven books, including Joe Public Doesn’t Care About Your Hospital and Joe Public 2030, his insights have shaped the future of healthcare marketing. A sought-after speaker and industry thought leader, Chris regularly presents at top conferences and contributes to leading publications.  

Chris holds a bachelor’s degree in journalism and mass communications from Iowa State University and an MBA from the University of St. Thomas in Minneapolis.

Victor Reiss Chief Consumer Officer, UNC Health LinkedIn

As Chief Consumer Officer at UNC Health, Victor Reiss leads enterprise strategy for consumer insights, growth marketing, brand, community engagement, and communications. Guided by his belief that the future of healthcare will be defined by how we honor people’s time, he sees every moment returned to patients, families, and communities as an opportunity for healing, connection, and living more fully. Previously, he served as System Vice President of Growth Marketing and Consumer Insights at UNC Health. He has also held leadership roles at Cancer Treatment Centers of America, and spent more than a decade at FedEx.

Victor is a Fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives, serves on the board of the UNC Health Physicians Network, and holds an MBA from Indiana Wesleyan University and an undergraduate degree from Butler University.

Matt Gove Chief Growth Officer, QuickMD LinkedIn

Matt Gove is Chief Growth Officer for QuickMD, one of the fastest-growing telehealth providers in the U.S. and the country’s largest provider of medication-assisted treatment for patients with opioid use disorder. He previously served as Chief Marketing Officer for Summit Health-CityMD before its $9 billion acquisition by VillageMD in 2023, and as Chief Consumer Officer for Piedmont Healthcare, where his team established a reputation as a national leader in consumer-centric marketing and digital experience design.

Earlier, Matt was a core member of the executive team that engineered an $80 million operational turnaround and raised more than $300 million for capital improvements at Grady Health System, Atlanta's safety-net healthcare provider.

Learn more about session

BPDerrrs attending

Chelsea Rice AVP, Content & Engagement Strategy Emily Wurz Group Account Director Caroline Dicioccio VP, Strategy Taylor Clary VP, Principal Erin Steuban VP, Communications, Growth Emily Bennett Senior Manager, Brand
Chelsea Rice AVP, Content & Engagement Strategy LinkedIn

Chelsea is a seasoned content strategist with over a decade of experience shaping healthcare narratives across digital, traditional, and emerging platforms. Before joining BPD, she built her career as a journalist and digital strategist, bringing a multifaceted perspective to healthcare communications. At BPD, Chelsea wears many hats — storyteller, strategist, and editor — helping brands determine what stories to tell and how to effectively engage their audiences. She holds a Master of Public Health in Health Policy and Management from the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health and is deeply passionate about crafting narratives that resonate with communities and align with evolving health policy.

Outside of work, Chelsea enjoys playing tennis and is on a mission to visit all 50 states before turning 40.

 
Emily Wurz Group Account Director LinkedIn

Emily is a client service leader with over a decade of agency expertise, leading national and global accounts within a variety of industries. An expert on her clients’ business, Emily brings a balance of ambition and calm under pressure that makes her a trusted partner for both client and agency teams alike. After-hours, you can find Emily hiking the Tennessee trails with her sock-eating pup Tilly or restaurant-hopping through Nashville, looking for the spiciest items on the menu.

Caroline Dicioccio VP, Strategy LinkedIn

As a VP, Strategy at BPD, Caroline helps brands connect more deeply with their audiences through insight-driven campaigns that change minds and inspire hearts. A strategist and creative partner, she guides organizations to better understand their environments and culture, translating those insights into purposeful, effective marketing. She has delivered award-winning work for some of the world’s largest consumer and healthcare brands, earning SABRE and Effie recognition along the way.

With a background in behavioral science and cultural anthropology—and a healthy obsession with emerging trends—Caroline blends rigor and curiosity to uncover the human truths that drive impact.

Caroline is passionate about floristry for all the same reasons she's a strategist—bringing colorful pieces together to create a balanced, inspiring whole!

Taylor Clary VP, Principal LinkedIn

Taylor brings 18 years of account service experience in brand and shopper marketing. Most recently, she worked at TMA (The Marketing Arm), where she helped build the State Farm and Frito-Lay brands.

Outside of work, Taylor enjoys cooking with her 2.5-year-old son, Sullivan, attending concerts with her husband, Ryan, and watching Bravo with her Shorkie, Margaux. She is always planning her next trip, with some of her favorite destinations including Ireland to see a Notre Dame game (Go Irish!) and Japan, where she celebrated Christmas with her entire family.

Erin Steuban VP, Communications, Growth LinkedIn

Erin holds a Bachelor’s degree in Communication from Nebraska Wesleyan University and has over 12 years of experience in transforming healthcare experiences. With a strong passion for utilizing data and technology to enhance brand strategies, Erin excels in building relationships, identifying clients’ most critical challenges, and aligning tailored solutions to drive success.

Outside of her professional work, Erin enjoys spending time with her husband, two children, and their miniature dachshund. Her personal interests include travel, Taylor Swift, and NASCAR, reflecting her diverse passions and dynamic approach to both life and work.

Emily Bennett Senior Manager, Brand

As Senior Manager of Brand Events, she leads end-to-end planning and execution for conferences and company-sponsored events, ensuring seamless logistics and impactful brand activations.
She manages the company’s presence at industry conferences—from sponsorships and speaker coordination to exhibit booth logistics and team participation. She also oversees external event planning, including venue selection, vendor negotiation, on-site execution, and budget management.

In addition to live events, she supports webinar logistics, promotional materials, and branded swag production. She collaborates with marketing and business development teams to support pitch efforts, manage budgets, and amplify brand visibility across all touchpoints.

The Future of the CMO

Marketing’s value in health systems is under siege.

What began as a quiet elimination of CMO roles across top health systems is now a full-blown reckoning: healthcare economics, financial pressures, and the rise of AI are forcing healthcare marketers to face new realities, or face dwindling influence, budgets and job security.

The Courage to Save Millions

Health system leaders are always on the lookout for ideas and strategies from outside the industry to advance their organization.

Here’s one worth a closer look, a strategy that strengthens culture, enhances reputation and market share, and can add millions to the bottom line. It hinges on a single decision made at the right time, one that most leaders overlook during mergers and acquisitions.

With deep expertise in social-first, full-funnel marketing, she guides organizations toward marketing maturity and consumer-first engagement strategies.

With a decade of experience spanning healthcare and consumer brands, Stephanie has led digital and content strategy for organizations like MD Anderson Cancer Center, Cincinnati Children’s, Johns Hopkins Medicine, Delta Air Lines, Amazon, and Walmart. Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy LinkedIn

Emily is a former healthcare business reporter turned content strategist responsible for building marketing plans and engagement strategies that connect brands to their audiences. She loves to cook, read and travel, and in her spare time, she teaches hot yoga and takes her unbelievably energetic dog to the dog park.

Regan McFarland Senior Content Creator LinkedIn

As a Content Creator at BPD, Regan believes in the power of great storytelling—and brings a unique blend of strategy and production to every brand she touches. She leads social strategy and influencer marketing for clients like the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Cincinnati Children’s, Henry Ford Health, and UNC Health, while also producing content across channels, from short films and typography animations to photography and social-first campaigns.

Prior to BPD, Regan spent four years at Acadia Healthcare producing video content for over 100 facilities nationwide. A graduate of Florida State University with a degree in Digital Media Production and a minor in Entrepreneurship, she has built a career around social-first health marketing.

Briana Carozza Marketing Director LinkedIn

She leads efforts to develop innovative, results-focused campaigns that connect with audiences and deliver measurable impact for clients.

With a bachelor’s degree in Business Administration and Management from Marist University, Briana brings a proven track record of translating complex goals into actionable strategies that enhance engagement and performance. Her approach combines analytical insight with creative vision, positioning brands for long-term success. A dedicated member of the BPD BS&G team, Briana is committed to advancing the company’s mission and strengthening BPD’s position as a bold, innovative leader in healthcare marketing.

We want to hear from the leaders pushing boundaries — from new models of care and technology that empower patients, to fresh thinking about how hospitals and health systems can innovate. We'll be sharing our perspective, but more importantly, we’ll be listening. September 18, 2025, 9:30 AM CT

Tomorrow is Too Late: Time is Running Out for Health Systems to Validate Their Value

Hospitals and health systems have long been targeted by negative narratives that portray them as profit-driven entities prioritizing revenue over patient care. As insurance premiums rise and administrative burdens increase, blame for healthcare costs has shifted to hospitals. This narrative, fueled by media campaigns, academic research, and external pressures, has eroded consumer trust, with many believing hospitals care more about profits than patients. With mounting political and reputational risks, it's time for hospitals to unite and defend their commitment to quality patient care.

By the end of this session, attendees will be able to:

1

Promote the value of their hospitals and healthcare systems

2

Take a more strategic and proactive approach in defining their reputation within their communities

Identify strategies for rebuilding and maintaining trust with patients and the public

Five steps you can take now to build for the future

1

Assess your market analysis and add one new research input that goes beyond the VOC. Then package it up with your POV and share with your CEO.

2

Evaluate your span of control and pick a path. Then make a high-level, one-year plan on how to achieve it.

3

Commit to spending one new/additional hour using AI tools and track your activity. From there, expand and enhance where and how it can transform your everyday work.

4

No matter when you last had the conversation, sit down with your CEO and have them articulate or reiterate their top three expectations of you and your team.

title: Do You Really Need Matching Luggage? The Balance of Brand and Service Line Approaches
content:
There is a widespread assumption that brand and service line marketing should be "matched luggage," meaning they should look the same, use the same creative, and follow the same messaging. While this is often the goal and can be effective, it is not always the best path forward. This webinar will focus on those situations where you might benefit from different brand and service-line messaging and creative, and why. Learning Objectives:
  • Identify and discuss scenarios where the matched luggage approach is effective and where it might not be the best strategy.
  • Learn strategies for decoupling brand creative and messaging from service line marketing creative and messaging, allowing for a more powerful brand approach
  • Gain insights through examples and lessons learned from leading organizations that have used this approach successfully

title: Is Rome Burning? Let's Discuss the Changing CMO Role
content:
The value of marketing to health systems in a competitive landscape has always seemed obvious. Yet, shifting societal and industry dynamics threaten the very role of marketing in healthcare.

Growing demand from an aging population; reduced capacity due to long-term labor shortage issues; ongoing financial stress for many health systems; and artificial intelligence. All these things add up to an environment unforeseen in this space. How do CMOs demonstrate their impact on their organizations, and defend the fundamental value of marketing in this shifting landscape?

At the 2024 Joe Public Retreat, health system marketing leaders from across the country gathered to discuss the transformation of healthcare marketing and how marketers can prepare for what's ahead. Our expert panel will recap key insights from the exclusive event, including takeaways from expert-led panels and executive keynotes.

Learning Objectives:

The impact of AI on marketing

How the CMO role is evolving outside of the healthcare space

How shifting healthcare economics may impact marketing and the role of the CMO

Innovative approaches to align marketing with long-term organizational goals

title: What AMCs Can Learn From Luxury Brands
content:
AMCs can be seen as the luxury brand in their respective markets, and there’s much we can learn from their expansion stratagies.

 

Many AMCs are going through a profound change in their business model, expanding their offerings and value beyond their sweet spot of high-acuity, complex care. But in the process, they are encountering a wicked branding challenge: How do you expand your brand positioning to capture broader value without diluting your core differentiation?

Interestingly, AMCs can be seen as the luxury brand in their respective markets, and there’s much we can learn from how luxury brands have tried to expand their reach to mass market audiences to help AMCs tackle this difficult challenge.

Predictions we'll be discussing at HLTH

Five bold predictions for how consumers will engage healthcare in 2030 and beyond
Healthcare is in the midst of major industry shifts. BPD and its “team 2030” of healthcare
strategists, researchers, and futurists interviewed dozens of healthcare C-Suite leaders
to identify predictions for the future of healthcare — in 2030 and beyond.

The Copernican
Consumer

Healthcare will increasingly center around the consumer wherever they are —at work, shopping, at home, or online. Enabled by data, blockchain, AI, sensors and wearables, healthcare services will revolve around consumers, 24/7/365, with the potential to upend the existing place-based model of healthcare in the U.S.

Constricted
Consumerism

The theory of consumerism has not delivered on its promises. While patients will face a narrowing range of care options, particularly in critical and costly situations, the largest healthcare "consumers" – public and private payors – will continue to grow in power and restrict choice.

The Funnel
Wars

Retail, tech and other disruptors stand to reshape the healthcare landscape, with traditional health systems forced to broaden into health and wellness, or shrink into "downstream vendors of care." The battle will happen at the top of the acuity funnel — retail, urgent, emergency and primary care — with the patient relationship on the line.

Rise of the
Health Sects

Rising distrust in mainstream medicine, fueled by the pandemic and political polarization, will lead to the formation of distinct "health sects" that align with specific worldviews. The result could be the emergence of healthcare organizations oriented around political beliefs and practices.

Disparity
Dystopia

The gap between the healthcare "haves" and "have-nots" will continue to grow, as those with resources will enjoy the benefits of advanced medicine, while millions will feel the devastating effects of climate change, rising costs, the technology gap and shrinking necessities such as food, water and clean air.

Staying the Course on Health Equity Amid Murky DEI Terrain

Tuesday | October 15 | 8:30-9:15 am In healthcare, our mission is to improve healthcare outcomes for all, but this is one of many uphill battles our industry navigates. Throughout the last few years, the country has in earnest begun to reckon with a long history of racial inequities, and the pandemic changed the entire nation’s perception of what it means to achieve equity in healthcare. Organizations have been working to right the ship for minoritized patients who have been chronically underserved and ensuring that the organization is better supporting employees who have been historically overlooked. In addition, employee expectations have changed, and they are more vocal about wanting the organizations they work for to foster a sense of belongingness that can result in better employee performance, and they are choosing with their feet. These efforts have been a worthy challenge on their own, but there is a new added layer of difficulty amid recent state legislative decisions on equitable access to care, making it more difficult or unlawful for some patients to access care. How can health systems navigate and lead this complex work and best communicate these efforts to patients, employees, and partners while minimizing public concern? Join BPD and Children’s Hospital Association as they discuss insights and advice on how to champion Health Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion (HEDI) by addressing social drivers of health for patient populations. Learning objectives for this session are:
  • Learn how to continue to make progress on your HEDI efforts despite the environmental pressures.
  • Learn unique strategies and language that will enable you to share your health equity efforts with your community and stakeholders.

title: Join this webinar to hear five bold predictions shaping the future of health.
content:
According to the book Joe Public 2030, there are five key ways consumer health engagement may change over the coming decade, covering everything from AI and personal monitoring to consumerism, new competition, the politicization of healthcare and growing health disparities. Join this webinar to hear five bold predictions about that future, which range from exciting and promising to ominous and discouraging.

Based on insights developed by a team of researchers, strategists and futurists at BPD, the five core predictions are supported by more than 250 resource citations and input from 22 industry experts who were interviewed, including health system CEOs, venture capitalists, entrepreneurs and physicians. For example, contributors include the CEOs of Geisinger and Henry Ford Health System, the head of the healthcare sector at Bain Capital, the founder of Sesame Health (and current board chair for the Leapfrog Group), the head of brand at CVS Health and the CMOs at Cleveland Clinic, Mercy Bon Secours, Johns Hopkins, Cincinnati Children’s and Intermountain.

The goal of this webinar is to spark conversation about how the future of health and healthcare in the U.S. might emerge, and how individuals and organizations might want to prepare for – or even change – that future.
